claim: LLM benchmarks have become increasingly divorced from real-world coding productivity; better tools and systems on top of LLMs may account for more improvement than the underlying model
actionable: When evaluating models for your coding tasks, don't rely solely on benchmarks; run real-world tests with your actual tools and workflows; consider that 50% of improvements may come from tooling, not the model
source_title: Is Gemini 3 Really the Best AI Ever?

claim: Plan mode (having agents explore codebase and create engineering plan before implementation) is effective for larger companies handling complex code changes with multiple dependencies
actionable: For complex refactoring or multi-file changes, use plan mode to have the AI understand architecture first; follow with implementation mode to execute the planned changes
source_title: Is Gemini 3 Really the Best AI Ever?

claim: Built-in browser integration (like anti-gravity's Google Chrome integration) enables agents to verify UI changes by autonomously browsing and screenshotting live websites
actionable: Use IDE browser integrations to have agents autonomously verify their UI changes by navigating sites, taking screenshots, and providing visual feedback on design improvements
source_title: Is Gemini 3 Really the Best AI Ever?

claim: Cline-Bench (real-world LLM evaluation using GitHub repositories and actual engineering tasks) is a better approach to evaluating coding models than synthetic benchmarks
actionable: When evaluating LLMs for coding, look for real-world benchmarks that measure performance on actual GitHub tasks; consider using Cline-Bench or similar real-task evaluation frameworks
source_title: Is Gemini 3 Really the Best AI Ever?

claim: Model speed (tokens per second) may be more important than model quality for coding agents, as faster iteration enables more attempts in the same time window
actionable: When choosing between a slightly better but slower model versus a faster but slightly weaker model for agents, consider that agent iteration speed compounds and may favor speed
source_title: Is Gemini 3 Really the Best AI Ever?
